meta.description: Browser WebAR over Al Fahidi: floating place labels, marker tracking, and then-and-now photo overlays — no app required.

hero.headline: Raise your phone. Watch the district breathe.
hero.intro: A digital layer over the alleys of Al Fahidi: place labels float around you, and old photographs of Dubai settle over the scene in front of you — all inside the browser, with nothing to install.

live.title: Live AR — camera
live.desc: Your phone's camera and compass become a magic window: point the phone around the district and floating labels reveal what you are looking at. Scan the printed Al Fahidi marker to anchor a 3D wind tower and an old-photo alignment card.
live.note: Best on a phone over HTTPS (localhost works for development).
live.unavailable: Camera access is not available in this browser — open the simulator instead.

sim.title: Simulator — no camera
sim.desc: A panoramic sweep of the district with the same floating labels and info hotspots. Built for projectors, juries and desktops — the demo never depends on a camera.
sim.note: Works on any device: drag the scene, use the arrow keys, or the angle slider.

how.heading: How it works
how.step1: Pick a mode: the camera on your phone, or the simulator on any screen.
how.step2: Allow camera and motion-sensor access when the browser asks.
how.step3: Move your device — or drag the scene — to explore the floating labels and open their stories.
how.step4: Print the Al Fahidi marker (or show it on a second screen) and scan it in live AR.

marker.heading: The Al Fahidi marker
marker.desc: A high-contrast wind-tower glyph. Print it on A5/A4 paper or display it on another screen, then scan it in live AR to summon the 3D barjeel and the old-photo card.
marker.alt: Printable AR marker: a geometric black, white and gold wind-tower glyph
marker.printHint: Matte paper and even lighting track best.

archive.heading: From the Dubai archive
archive.desc: Three public-domain photographs travel with the experience — blend them over the live scene with the before/after slider.
archive.sheikh: Sheikh Saeed and Sheikh Juma Al Maktoum, 1950

credits.heading: Photo credits
credits.body: Historical photographs via Wikimedia Commons, public domain: “Dubai Creek 1964” and “Al Ras, Deira, mid-1960s” by Noor Ali; “Sheikh Saeed and Sheikh Juma Al Maktoum, 1950”, photographer unknown.

live.title: Live AR
live.cameraErrorHint: Check the browser permission, make sure the page is served over HTTPS, or switch to the simulator.
live.motionHint: iOS asks for permission before sharing the device orientation.
live.dragHint: No compass? Drag the scene to look around.
live.cameraLabel: Live camera feed of your surroundings

marker3d.title: Marker tracking
marker3d.scan: Point the camera at the printed Al Fahidi marker
marker3d.missing: The compiled marker target is not part of this build — download and print the marker for later, or keep exploring in magic-window mode.
marker3d.failed: The tracking engine failed to load — check the connection and try again.

sim.title: District simulator
sim.hint: Drag the scene — or use the slider — to look around.
sim.panoramaMissing: The panoramic backdrop is unavailable — the labels still work.
sim.backdropAlt: Stylized panorama of the Al Fahidi skyline with wind towers

labels.empty: Could not load the places from the server — the scene stays navigable; try again later.
